raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
For March 2026, total spend of £20,928 came in £5,463 (+35.3%) above the budget of £15,465. The overrun is concentrated in two significant lines — Sales and Purchases — which together account for £5,753 of the significant-line variance, driven by a single accelerated deal.
The primary driver across both lines is the Meridian Group contract, originally forecast to close in mid-April 2026 but pulled forward by 22 days to 24 March at the customer's request to align with their own financial year-end. The deal (£4,800 ARR plus £1,200 one-time onboarding, totalling £6,000) landed entirely in March, producing the Sales outperformance. The associated procurement of deployment materials (£750) flowed through Purchases in the same period, creating a correlated cost overage. Both variances are expected to partially reverse in April, as recurring revenue normalises against budget and no further onboarding materials are anticipated — though this requires confirmation. All remaining variances are insignificant and are summarised at the foot of this report.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,003 (+83.4%) | Budget: £6,000 | Actual: £11,003
Sales exceeded budget by £5,003 (+83.4%), with actual revenue of £11,003 against a budget of £6,000.
The variance is largely attributable to the accelerated close of the Meridian Group contract (CRM ref: MER-001). Originally scheduled to close on 15 April 2026, the deal was pulled forward by 22 days to 24 March at Meridian's request to align with their financial year-end. The contract comprises £4,800 ARR plus a £1,200 one-time onboarding fee (total contract value stated at £5,100 in Slack, with £6,000 total value across ARR and onboarding per CRM). The MSA was formally executed on 24 March, with a revenue recognition memo attached and CFO countersignature on 25 March.
Note: The CRM records £4,800 ARR + £1,200 onboarding = £6,000 from Meridian, and Slack cites a contract value of £5,100 (one-time plus first month recurring). These figures may reflect different breakdowns of the same deal. The £5,003 budget variance is not fully reconciled to a single confirmed figure from the evidence alone — a revenue recognition review is recommended to confirm the precise in-month amount posted.
The remaining variance (approximately £1,003 above the Meridian contribution, depending on the recognised figure) likely reflects normal underlying trading; however, no separate context was found for this portion.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-03-24: @channel Q1 close update — Meridian contract signed today, ahead of April target. Deal pulled forward at customer's request to beat their FY end. Total contract value £5,100 (one-time plus first month recurring).
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-03-24-meridian: RE: Meridian Group — Contract execution. Signed MSA received from Meridian legal on 24 March. Revenue recognition memo attached. CFO countersigned 25 March.
- Calendar - 2024-11-05T22:15:00Z - calendar-sales-broad - cal-2026-03-24: Meridian contract signing — 14:00 BST. CFO and CRO in attendance. Scheduled at customer's request to align with their financial year close 31 March.
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Meridian Group — Stage: Closed Won. Close date moved from 15 April to 24 March. ARR: £4,800. One-time onboarding: £1,200. Pulled forward by 22 days.

Purchases | +£750 (+150.0%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £1,250
Purchases exceeded budget by £750 (+150.0%), with actual spend of £1,250 against a budget of £500.
The variance is directly linked to the Meridian Group onboarding. A stock order of £750 for deployment materials was raised on 26 March via the standard procurement channel with a PO attached, specifically to support Meridian's initial deployment. A COGS reconciliation memo confirms these direct materials were posted in-month and characterises the variance as expected given the revenue outperformance.
The base-budget purchases of £500 appear to reflect normal monthly procurement activity. The £750 Meridian materials order accounts for the full overage, and the correlated nature of this cost with the accelerated deal close means the variance is expected rather than unplanned. No further onboarding material spend is indicated in the evidence, though this may require confirmation once April's figures are available.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-09T12:15:00Z - slack-purchases-broad - ops-slack-2026-03-26: Stock order for Meridian onboarding raised — £750 materials for initial deployment. Standard procurement channel, PO attached.
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:15:00Z - gmail-purchases-broad - gmail-2026-03-26-cogs: COGS reconciliation for March — direct materials for Meridian onboarding posted in month. Variance is expected given revenue outperformance.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines each carried immaterial variances in March 2026 and did not meet the threshold for detailed investigation. No further commentary is provided, but any lines with a 100% variance (i.e., zero actual against a non-zero budget, or vice versa) may warrant a brief check to confirm no timing or coding issues exist.
- Advertising & Marketing: +£45 (+0.6%)
- Audit & Accountancy Fees: -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees: -£30 (-100.0%)
- Cleaning: -£100 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 0%: -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 100% Business: +£18 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses: +£124 (+103.6%)
- Light Power Heating: +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses: -£6 (-4.8%)
- Postage Freight Courier: -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing Stationery: +£46 (+227.9%)
- Rent: -£46 (-4.4%)
- Repairs Maintenance: -£100 (-100.0%)
- Subscriptions: £0 (0.0%)
- Telephone Internet: -£2 (-4.6%)
- Travel National: -£150 (-100.0%)
